2604.20727.title: Supplement Generation Training for Enhancing Agentic Task Performance
2604.20727.paper_summary: Training large foundation models for agentic tasks is increasingly impractical due to the high computational costs, long iteration cycles, and rapid obsolescence as new models are continuously released. Instead of post-training massive models for every new task or domain, we propose Supplement Generation Training (SGT), a more efficient and sustainable strategy. SGT trains a smaller LLM to generate useful supplemental text that, when appended to the original input, helps the larger LLM solve the task more effectively. These lightweight models can dynamically adapt supplements to task requirements, improving performance without modifying the underlying large models. This approach decouples task-specific optimization from large foundation models and enables more flexible, cost-effective deployment of LLM-powered agents in real-world applications.
2604.20727.paper_summary_zh: 訓練大型基礎模型以應對代理任務變得越來越不切實際，因為計算成本高、迭代周期長，且隨著新模型不斷發布，快速過時。<lb/>我們提出補充生成訓練（SGT），這是一種更高效且可持續的策略，而不是對每個新任務或領域進行後期訓練的大型模型。<lb/>SGT 訓練一個較小的 LLM 來生成有用的補充文本，當這些文本附加到原始輸入時，有助於更大 LLM 更有效地解決任務。<lb/>這些輕量級模型可以根據任務需求動態調整補充內容，提升性能而無需修改基礎的大型模型。<lb/>這種方法將任務特定的優化與大型基礎模型解耦，並使 LLM 驅動的代理在現實應用中能夠更靈活、具成本效益地部署。
2604.20727.author: Young Min Cho et.al.
2604.20727.authors: Young Min Cho, Daniele Bonadiman, Divya Bhargavi, Tamer Alkhouli, Salvatore Romeo, Dongwei Jiang, Khushbu Pahwa, Yubin Ge, Etsuko Ishii, Monica Sunkara, Yi Zhang
